# Orchestrator-plus-sub-skills architecture

The project is structured as an orchestrator skill (`rhdh`) that presents an intake menu and routes to specialized sub-skills (`overlay`, `rhdh-local`, `create-plugin`) rather than a single monolithic skill. The `create-plugin` skill itself uses a sub-command router for its four concerns (backend, frontend, export, wiring). Most Agent Skills are single-purpose — this one is a router. The orchestrator handles orientation (environment detection, doctor checks, config) and delegates domain-specific work to sub-skills that can be developed and tested independently. This adds complexity (intake menus, routing tables, cross-skill references) but enables progressive disclosure: agents load only the sub-skill relevant to the current task instead of the entire corpus.
